screenshot_note: Puppeteer not available in this environment — code review only
theme: Two of the three-review bugs are finally dead; five survivors and two brand-new accessibility ghosts haunt the codebase
visual_observations: isLoneSurvivor (Office.jsx:1230) NOW has the error guard: `&& nonMgr.filter(w => w.status === 'error').length === 0`. Fixed after three consecutive reviews flagging it.
SOLO_SPOTLIGHT (App.jsx:1310) NOW has `&& activeWorkers[0].status !== 'error'` guard. Hero spotlight no longer fires for erroring workers.

Bloberto click (Office.jsx:1568) STILL passes `onClick={handleCharClick}` with no bloberto guard. handleCharClick (Office.jsx:1248) calls onWorkerClick(worker) unconditionally. App.jsx:803 sets focusedWorker to 'bloberto' — no WorkerCard exists for it. Fourth consecutive review flagging.

Wandering workers (Office.jsx:1595) STILL `idleWorkers.filter(w => !meetingWorkers.includes(w))` — reference equality. meetingWorkerIds Set at line 1223 is unused here. Fourth consecutive review flagging.
wb-task-count (Office.jsx:1395) STILL `style={{ fontSize: '0.4rem' }}` — sub-pixel on most screens. Fourth consecutive review flagging.
workerConfetti (App.jsx:1173) STILL `Array.from({ length: 30 }, ...)` inline allocation. CONFETTI_PIECES module-scope pattern exists at line 35 and is unused here. Fourth consecutive review flagging.
quoteBonus (App.jsx:1449) STILL loops silently at BONUS_TAGLINES.length with `p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 1 : p + 1`. No completion ceremony. Fourth consecutive review flagging.
VibeSparkline (App.jsx:426-427) has BOTH aria-label on the SVG element AND a <title> child element — same double-announcement pattern that WallClock had in review 90 and was fixed. NEW finding.

activityByWorker (App.jsx:960) slices to `.slice(-3)` per worker before passing to WorkerCard as activityEntries. WorkerCard (App.jsx:312) then does `.slice(-5).reverse()` expecting up to 5 entries. Card back always shows ≤3 items regardless of actual log depth. NEW finding.

Conference table (Office.jsx:1021 and 1039) both seated-worker render blocks declare `const cx = 52, orbitR = 38` and then compute `y = cx - orbitR * Math.cos(rad)` — using cx where cy should be used. Because outer cx and cy are both 52 (line 936) the result is numerically correct but the variable name shadow would silently break if cx and cy ever diverge. NEW finding.

WallClock (Office.jsx:1131) has `pointerEvents: 'auto'` in inline style but no click handler. The clock sits at position left:22%, top:2.5% and intercepts pointer events over office floor content behind it. No user feedback. NEW finding.

fixed_since_last_review.title: isLoneSurvivor Error Guard Added — Fixed After Three Reviews
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: Office.jsx:1230 — added `&& nonMgr.filter(w => w.status === 'error').length === 0`. Lone survivor label no longer appears when an error worker is visible alongside the solo working worker.

fixed_since_last_review.title: SOLO_SPOTLIGHT No Longer Fires for Error-Status Workers — Fixed After Three Reviews
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx:1310 — added `&& activeWorkers[0].status !== 'error'` to the solo spotlight condition. Hero messaging no longer renders while the worker's screen is flashing PANIC.

ideas.title: VibeSparkline Double-Announces to Screen Readers — New Finding
ideas.description: App.jsx:426-427 — the sparkline SVG has `aria-label={...}` on the `<svg>` element AND a `<title>` child element. Screen readers announce both. WallClock had this same pattern and it was fixed in review 90 (removed the `<title>`, kept `aria-label`). Apply the exact same fix here: remove the `<title>` element from VibeSparkline, leave the `aria-label`. The individual circle `<title>` tooltips at line 432 are fine — they're inside child elements, not on the root SVG. WHY: double-announcement makes the sparkline unreadable to assistive technology users, and the fix pattern is already established in this codebase.

ideas.title: activityByWorker Slices to 3 But WorkerCard Expects 5 — New Finding
ideas.description: App.jsx:960 — `map.set(w.name, activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === w.name).slice(-3))`. This caps the passed activityEntries at 3. App.jsx:312 — WorkerCard then does `.slice(-5).reverse()` expecting up to 5 entries. Since the input is already capped at 3, the card back always shows at most 3 history items regardless of how much history exists. Fix: change `.slice(-3)` at line 960 to `.slice(-5)` to match the card's intent. OR change the card to `.slice(-3)` if 3 was intentional. Either way, the values must agree. WHY: the card visually promises 5 slots of history but silently shows fewer due to upstream truncation the reader cannot see.

ideas.title: Bloberto Click Ghost-Navigates to Dashboard — Fourth Review Flagging
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1568 — Bloberto has `onClick={handleCharClick}`. handleCharClick (Office.jsx:1248) calls `onWorkerClick(worker)` unconditionally. App.jsx:803 — handleWorkerClick sets tab to 'dashboard' and focusedWorker to 'bloberto'. No WorkerCard exists for bloberto so the 3-second focus timer fires silently. Fix: in handleCharClick (Office.jsx:1248), add `if (worker.id === 'bloberto') return` before calling onWorkerClick, OR give Bloberto a distinct onClick that shows a manager ping/toast without navigating. WHY: clicking the most prominent character in the office should not silently navigate away with no visible result. This is the fourth consecutive review.

ideas.title: Conference Table Seated-Worker y Coordinate Uses cx Instead of cy — New Finding
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1021-1023 (meetingWorkers render) and 1039-1041 (standupWorkers render) — both blocks declare `const cx = 52, orbitR = 38` and compute `const y = cx - orbitR * Math.cos(rad)`. They should use `cy` but shadow it with `cx`. Currently cx === cy === 52 (line 936) so the math is correct — but this is a latent bug. If the table is ever made non-circular (cx ≠ cy), seated character positions will be calculated incorrectly. Fix: rename inner declarations to `const tableCx = 52, tableOrbitR = 38` in both blocks, and compute `const y = tableCx - tableOrbitR * Math.cos(rad)` — or better, hoist into a shared helper. WHY: code that only works by coincidence is a time bomb.

ideas.title: Wandering Workers .includes() Object Reference — Fourth Review Flagging
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1595 — `idleWorkers.filter(w => !meetingWorkers.includes(w))` uses object reference equality. meetingWorkerIds Set exists at line 1223 and is correctly used for coffeeWorkers filtering. Fix: change line 1595 to `idleWorkers.filter(w => !meetingWorkerIds.has(w.id))`. The fix pattern exists one line above in the same file. WHY: fourth consecutive review. The meeting worker Set is there specifically for this purpose and is simply not being used.

ideas.title: WallClock pointerEvents:auto With No Handler Silently Eats Clicks
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1131 — WallClock SVG has `pointerEvents: 'auto'` in inline style but no click handler, no aria role, and no cursor feedback. The clock sits at left:22%, top:2.5% and intercepts pointer events over the office floor behind it. Users clicking near the top of the office may find their click swallowed by the clock. Fix: either add `pointerEvents: 'none'` (the clock is purely decorative), or add a click handler that surfaces the CLOCK_COMMENTS string as a toast (a low-effort delight). WHY: invisible click traps erode trust in UI interactions.

ideas.title: wb-task-count at 0.4rem Is Below Legibility Threshold — Fourth Review Flagging
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1395 — `style={{ fontSize: '0.4rem' }}` renders at ~6.4px. Below 10px minimum for incidental copy. Fix: raise to 0.6rem or move into the whiteboard's aria-label and remove the visible element entirely. WHY: fourth consecutive review. Information rendered at sub-pixel size is worse than absent — it implies the UI is broken. The fix is one number.

ideas.title: workerConfetti Inline Allocation — Fourth Review Flagging, Fix Pattern Exists
ideas.description: App.jsx:1173 — `Array.from({ length: 30 }, (_, i) => (<span .../>))` allocated inline in JSX every render during the 2500ms confetti window. CONFETTI_PIECES at line 35 is the exact same pattern hoisted to module scope. Fix: create `WORKER_CONFETTI_PIECES` constant at module scope using same pattern, apply color via CSS custom property or className on the parent div. WHY: fourth consecutive review. The fix is a 4-line copy from line 35.

ideas.title: quoteBonus Completion Has No Ceremony — Fourth Review Flagging
ideas.description: App.jsx:1449 — `p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 1 : p + 1` loops silently. The counter hides at boundary and resets. Fix: on first full cycle, fire completionToast with golden styling (`border: '2px solid #fbbf24'`) and set a localStorage flag. After the flag is set, show a permanent ✦ after the version string. WHY: fourth consecutive review. The tagline hunt is the only discovery mechanic in the footer. Its ending must land.

ideas.title: Group Ping: Wake the Whole Office at Once
ideas.description: Currently clicking a character pings one worker and shows their character-voice reaction. Wild idea: add a hidden 'all-hands ping' — triple-clicking Bloberto (or a long-press on mobile) triggers ALL characters in the office to show their ping reactions simultaneously, staggered by 200ms each, with Bloberto's reaction last. The result looks like Bloberto sent a company-wide message and everyone reacted. Uses existing PING_REACTIONS map — no new content needed. Store in localStorage to prevent spamming (cooldown of 60s). WHY: the office feels alive in isolated moments; a synchronized ensemble reaction would make it feel like a real place for the first time.
